title: Do Safety Guardrails Need to Reason? LeanGuard: A Fast and Light Approach for Robust Moderation
abstract: arXiv:2606.26686v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: In order to screen a prompt or a response, the recent guardrail methods generate a chain-of-thought (CoT) before they issue a verdict. This design follows a common belief that step-by-step reasoning improves a decision. However, CoT also makes the guard heavy and slow, because the model must generate many tokens before it decides. This may not match how guardrails are actually deployed. A guardrail sometimes should not be heavy and slow, and it often runs on-device, for example on an embodied robot. In this paper, we pose a question whether a safety guardrail really needs to reason. To answer this question, we train a lightweight bidirectional encoder and a reasoning guard on the same corpus, and we then remove only the reasoning while we keep everything else fixed. With this controlled same-base comparison, we show that the chain does not improve moderation accuracy. We name the resulting guard LeanGuard. A 395M label-only encoder reach

Title: Do Safety Guardrails Need to Reason? LeanGuard: A Fast and Light Approach for Robust Moderation
Abstract: arXiv:2606.26686v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: In order to screen a prompt or a response, the recent guardrail methods generate a chain-of-thought (CoT) before they issue a verdict. This design follows a common belief that step-by-step reasoning improves a decision. However, CoT also makes the guard heavy and slow, because the model must generate many tokens before it decides. This may not match how guardrails are actually deployed. A guardrail sometimes should not be heavy and slow, and it often runs on-device, for example on an embodied robot. In this paper, we pose a question whether a safety guardrail really needs to reason. To answer this question, we train a lightweight bidirectional encoder and a reasoning guard on the same corpus, and we then remove only the reasoning while we keep everything else fixed. With this controlled same-base comparison, we show that the chain does not improve moderation accuracy. We name the resulting guard LeanGuard. A 395M label-only encoder reach
translated_title: 安全护栏需要推理吗？LeanGuard：一种快速轻量的鲁棒审核方法
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.26686v1 公告类型：新 摘要：为了筛选提示或响应，最近的护栏方法在做出裁决前会生成思维链（CoT）。这种设计遵循一个普遍信念，即逐步推理能提升决策质量。然而，CoT也使护栏变得沉重且缓慢，因为模型必须在决策前生成大量标记。这可能不符合护栏实际部署的方式。护栏有时不应沉重缓慢，它通常运行在设备上，例如在具身机器人上。本文提出一个问题：安全护栏是否真的需要推理？为回答此问题，我们在同一语料库上训练了一个轻量级双向编码器和一个推理护栏，然后仅移除推理部分，同时保持其他所有设置不变。通过这种受控的相同基线比较，我们证明思维链并未提高审核准确性。我们将生成的护栏命名为LeanGuard。一个395M的纯标签编码器即可达到效果。
tldr: 本文通过受控实验证明，安全护栏中的思维链推理并不提升审核准确性，并提出了无需推理的轻量级LeanGuard模型，实现了快速高效的鲁棒审核。
en_tdlr: This paper demonstrates through controlled experiments that chain-of-thought reasoning in safety guardrails does not improve moderation accuracy, and proposes LeanGuard, a lightweight model without reasoning, achieving fast and efficient robust moderation.
